description: Apply the full Framer design system to the Ask OSAI page and its sub-components, ensuring every visual element follows the DESIGN.md spec: poster-grade display type with aggressive negative tracking, dark canvas, white pill CTAs, charcoal surfaces, gradient spotlight workflow cards, and the Inter Variable OpenType features.
steps: Review the current DESIGN.md and any files added by the getdesign CLI in FEAT-001. Understand what tokens are now available.

Update the Ask page empty state heading section in `app/ask/page.tsx`: Make the title use poster-grade display typography with aggressive negative tracking matching DESIGN.md display-lg (62px, -3.1px tracking, weight 600, line-height 1.0). Use the `.display-lg` or equivalent class.

Update the hero composer (`.ask-composer-hero` in globals.css) to fully embody the Framer spec: use surface-2 (#1c1c1c) background, 20px border-radius (rounded.xl from DESIGN.md), subtle level-2 elevation shadow with rgba(255,255,255,0.10) 0.5px top edge highlight.

Update the mode tabs (`.ask-mode-tab` in globals.css) to exactly match the Framer pricing-tab pattern: default = canvas background with ink-muted text; selected = surface-2 background with ink text; pill shape (100px radius); padding 8px 14px matching DESIGN.md button-translucent spec.

Transform the three recommended workflow cards (`.ask-mode-card` in globals.css) into gradient spotlight cards following the DESIGN.md gradient-spotlight-card spec. The three cards should use the three gradient variants: violet (radial-gradient with #6a4cf5), magenta (#d44df0), and orange (#ff7a3d). Use rounded.xl (20px) corners, 32px padding, white text. Update the page.tsx to apply distinct gradient classes to each card.

Update the Send button styling in the composer to match DESIGN.md button-primary: white background, black text, pill shape, font-weight 600. The disabled state should be opacity 0.4. This should already be handled by the shadcn Button component with default variant, but confirm it renders correctly.

Update the conversation-state message bubbles in `components/ask/message-bubble.tsx`: user messages should use a subtle surface-1 background with ink text and rounded.xl corners. Assistant messages should use a card with surface-1 background, hairline border, and rounded.xl corners. The OSAI avatar badge should remain white pill with black text.

Update citation chips in `components/ask/citation-chip.tsx` to use the Framer component language: surface-1 background, hairline border, pill radius (100px), caption-size type (13px, -0.13px tracking).
Update action cards in `components/ask/action-card.tsx` to use Framer card styling: surface-1 background, hairline border, rounded.lg (15px), and the approve button as a white pill primary CTA.

Update the eyebrow, subtext, disclaimer, section-label, and expectation CSS classes in the .ask-* section of globals.css to precisely match DESIGN.md typography tokens: eyebrow = micro (12px), subtext = body-lg (18px, -0.18px), disclaimer = micro (12px), section-label = caption (13px, -0.13px), expectations = body-sm (14px, -0.14px).
Ensure all spacing follows the Framer 5px-base grid from DESIGN.md: component gaps at 15px/20px/30px, card padding at 20-32px, section spacing at generous values.
Run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` to verify everything compiles correctly after all changes.
acceptance_criteria: The Ask page title uses poster-grade display typography (>=40px font-size, letter-spacing <= -2px, weight 600, line-height ~1.0)
The hero composer uses surface-2 background, 20px radius, and subtle elevation styling
Mode tabs follow the pricing-tab-default/selected pattern: pill shape, surface lift for active state
The three workflow cards use distinct gradient spotlight backgrounds (violet, magenta, orange) with white text, 20px+ radius, and 32px padding
Message bubbles use dark surface backgrounds with proper border radius (not rounded-full circles)
Citation chips use pill radius and surface-1 background
Action cards use Framer card styling with surface-1 and hairline borders
All text elements follow the correct DESIGN.md typography token sizes and tracking values
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/OSAI/osai-web && npm run typecheck` and confirm exit code 0
Run `cd /projects/sandbox/OSAI/osai-web && npm run build` and confirm exit code 0
